name: Ramp
description: Corporate card, spend management, and finance automation platform with APIs for cards, transactions, bills, reimbursements, and integrations.

auth_methods.note: Docs state Ramp supports OAuth 2.0, issues access tokens (used as Bearer tokens) and that client ID/secret authenticate to the token endpoint typically via HTTP Basic Auth.

credential_access.note: Getting started guide describes creating an application in the developer dashboard and receiving a client ID and secret; sandbox access can be requested from the dashboard. No docs page indicates human approval or sales/partnership requirement to obtain developer credentials.

api_surface.note: API reference describes a RESTful v1 surface covering Transactions, Cards, Users, Bills, Reimbursements, Custom Records, Webhooks, Applications and provides an OpenAPI spec.

buildability.note: Public docs describe OAuth flows, how to obtain client credentials via the dashboard, and a REST API reference with OpenAPI; sandbox environment is available for testing. Therefore one can obtain credentials and begin development without partner gating.

queries: site:docs.ramp.com authentication authorization client_id client secret
site:ramp.com API developer dashboard signup api key
